ddls:
CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the project */,
  "climate" TEXT /* Climate conditions of the project location */,
  "system_specs" TEXT /* Specifications of the solar-powered water pumping system */,
  "project_start_date" TEXT /* The start date of the project */,
  "project_end_date" TEXT /* The expected end date of the project */,
  "project_status" TEXT /* The current status of the project (e.g., Active, Completed) */,
  "project_budget" REAL /* The budget allocated for the project */,
  "project_manager" TEXT /* The name of the project manager */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The timestamp when the project was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* The timestamp when the project was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "solar_arrays" (
  "array_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each solar array */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the array belongs to */,
  "array_type" TEXT /* Type of solar array (e.g., monocrystalline, polycrystalline) */,
  "watts" REAL /* Power output of the solar array in watts */,
  "array_manufacturer" TEXT /* The manufacturer of the solar array */,
  "array_model" TEXT /* The model of the solar array */,
  "array_efficiency" REAL /* The efficiency of the solar array as a percentage */,
  "array_install_date" TEXT /* The date the solar array was installed */,
  "array_last_maintenance" TEXT /* The date of the last maintenance */,
  "array_notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the solar array */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("array_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_solar_arrays_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "motors" (
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each motor */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the motor belongs to */,
  "motor_type" TEXT /* Type of motor (e.g., DC, AC) */,
  "voltage" REAL /* Voltage rating of the motor */,
  "amps" REAL /* Current rating of the motor in amps */,
  "motor_manufacturer" TEXT /* The manufacturer of the motor */,
  "motor_model" TEXT /* The model of the motor */,
  "motor_efficiency" REAL /* The efficiency of the motor as a percentage */,
  "motor_install_date" TEXT /* The date the motor was installed */,
  "motor_last_maintenance" TEXT /* The date of the last maintenance */,
  "motor_notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the motor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("motor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_motors_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pumping_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pumping condition */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the condition belongs to */,
  "psi" REAL /* Pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI) */,
  "tdh_feet" REAL /* Total dynamic head in feet */,
  "tdh_meters" REAL /* Total dynamic head in meters */,
  "us_gpm" REAL /* Flow rate in US gallons per minute */,
  "lpm" REAL /* Flow rate in liters per minute */,
  "pump_manufacturer" TEXT /* The manufacturer of the pump */,
  "pump_model" TEXT /* The model of the pump */,
  "pump_install_date" TEXT /* The date the pump was installed */,
  "pump_last_maintenance" TEXT /* The date of the last maintenance */,
  "pump_notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the pump */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pumping_conditions_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_efficiencies" (
  "efficiency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each efficiency measurement */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the efficiency belongs to */,
  "efficiency" REAL /* Efficiency of the system as a percentage */,
  "efficiency_date" TEXT /* The date when the efficiency was measured */,
  "efficiency_notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the efficiency measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("efficiency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_efficiencies_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data log */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the log belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "psi" REAL /* Pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI) */,
  "tdh_feet" REAL /* Total dynamic head in feet */,
  "tdh_meters" REAL /* Total dynamic head in meters */,
  "us_gpm" REAL /* Flow rate in US gallons per minute */,
  "lpm" REAL /* Flow rate in liters per minute */,
  "motor_watts" REAL /* Motor power consumption in watts */,
  "solar_watts" REAL /* Solar power input in watts */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* The source of the log data (e.g., sensor, logger) */,
  "log_notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_logs_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* The username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* The password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* The role of the user (e.g., Admin, Project Manager, Analyst) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* The timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_users" (
  "project_user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project-user relationship */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the project */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the user */,
  "role_in_project" TEXT /* The role of the user in the project (e.g., Project Manager, Analyst) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The timestamp when the relationship was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* The timestamp when the relationship was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_project_users_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_project_users_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
